methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a Pre-Seed stage infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $3.4M to date. The comparable universe for this sector is limited, comprising two private companies (AiDash and Watoga Technologies) and no public peers, which constrains traditional peer-median approaches.

Given the sparse peer set, only the Stage-Progression method is applicable. This approach uses a $12.0B total raised proxy as a base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to Series A progression, yielding an implied valuation of $30.0B. The peer-median method cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the Infrastructure & Logistics sector—only one peer exists in the sector broadly, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical validity.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single methodological input, supports a valuation range of $25.5B (low) to $34.5B (high), with a median of $30.0B. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of a robust peer set; valuation credibility would materially improve with identification of additional Pre-Seed or Series A stage infrastructure companies. The stage-progression multiplier itself carries model risk, as the 2.5x assumption may not reflect sector-specific or company-specific capital efficiency dynamics.
